pi_items.source: task.json metadata and README.md line 25 - confirmed as the SQL view layer name the agent must create
setup_geoserver.sh creates 'ne' workspace and PostGIS datastore at lines 136-157
task.json metadata and README.md line 30 - confirmed as the SLD style name the agent must create
task.json metadata and task description line 4 - agent must set WFS title to 'Natural Earth WFS' which contains both keywords
task.json metadata, task description line 4, and README.md line 23 - all specify 5000 as the required WFS maxFeatures setting

task.json metadata, task description, and README.md line 26 all specify the same query. Verified via Natural Earth documentation: ne_populated_places is a real table with pop_max column documented at https://www.naturalearthdata.com/downloads/10m-cultural-vectors/10m-populated-places/

Code analysis: setup_geoserver.sh line 104 uses ogr2ogr with '-lco GEOMETRY_NAME=geom', which creates geometry column named 'geom'. However, README.md line 70 incorrectly claims the geometry column is 'wkb_geometry'. The actual database has 'geom'.

Natural Earth documentation confirms ne_110m_populated_places contains point geometries representing populated places/cities. Verified via https://www.naturalearthdata.com/ and https://github.com/nvkelso/natural-earth-vector
setup_geoserver.sh line 106 uses '-a_srs EPSG:4326'. WGS84/EPSG:4326 is the standard CRS for Natural Earth data, verified via https://epsg.io/4326 and https://www.naturalearthdata.com/

pi_items.verified_value: Natural Earth public domain dataset from naturalearthdata.com
pi_items.source: setup_geoserver.sh imports from /home/ga/natural_earth/ne_110m_populated_places.shp. Natural Earth is a real, public domain vector and raster map dataset available at https://www.naturalearthdata.com/ at 1:10m, 1:50m, and 1:110m scales.

Natural Earth documentation explicitly lists pop_max as a standard field used for sizing city labels. Documented at https://www.naturalearthdata.com/downloads/10m-cultural-vectors/10m-populated-places/
task.json metadata, task description, and README.md all specify the same filter threshold for identifying 'major cities'

privileged_info_summary: The task configures GeoServer WFS service with a filtered SQL view of the Natural Earth ne_110m_populated_places dataset (public domain). The source data is a real dataset from https://www.naturalearthdata.com/ containing point geometries representing worldwide populated places. The SQL view filters for pop_max > 1,000,000 (major cities) using valid SQL against the real ne_populated_places table. WFS title should contain 'Natural Earth' and 'WFS', maxFeatures should be set to 5000, and an SLD style 'city_marker' with red circle marks should be created and applied. All spatial reference systems use SRID 4326 (WGS84). Critical note: README incorrectly claims source geometry column is 'wkb_geometry' when setup_geoserver.sh actually creates it as 'geom'.
